title: Start Here
description: How to customize your new Pirate Social site

blocks.heading: How To Set Up Your Site
blocks.subheading: Use this sample page as a guide, then replace it with your own content.

blocks.body:
## 1) Edit this page

Open **/admin** and go to **Pages -> Start Here**.

You can add, remove, and reorder blocks to build custom pages.

## 2) Publish your first post

Go to **Posts** in the CMS and create a new post with a title, publish date, and content.

## 3) Configure your profile

Open **Settings** to set your site title, description, avatar, and social options.

## 4) Remove sample content

When you're ready, delete this page and the sample post from the CMS.

## 5) Use your own domain (optional)

Point your domain at your host (GitHub Pages, Netlify, or Vercel each have a
"custom domain" setting — no code involved). That's it: the first time anyone
visits, the site announces its new address to the Pirate network
automatically, and signing in from the site registers it too.

If social features show a connection message right after the move, wait a
minute and reload — the network is learning your new address. Signing out and
back in also refreshes everything, including where your posts feed from.

## If something looks stuck

- **"Couldn't reach the Pirate hub"** — usually a just-moved domain. Wait a
  minute, reload, and sign in again.
- **CMS won't open** — /admin needs the correct repo in its config; if you
  used the setup wizard this is already done.
- **New posts not in the network feed** — sign in once from your site so your
  feed address updates to your current domain.
